report_text: Patient developed severe skin rash approximately two days after initiating Paracetamol therapy for fever management.
Female patient reported upper GI bleeding with hematemesis after seven days of Ibuprofen use for chronic back pain.
Elderly male on long-term Warfarin anticoagulation presented with sudden onset severe headache and confusion. CT scan confirmed intracranial hemorrhage.
Young female experienced acute anaphylactic reaction within 15 minutes of first dose of Amoxicillin. Treated with epinephrine in emergency department.
Patient on Atorvastatin 40mg daily presented with severe muscle pain and dark urine. CK levels markedly elevated. Diagnosis of rhabdomyolysis confirmed.
Diabetic female on Metformin 1000mg twice daily developed nausea, vomiting and weakness. Laboratory findings consistent with lactic acidosis.
Male patient developed spontaneous Achilles tendon rupture after 10 days of Ciprofloxacin therapy for urinary tract infection.
Elderly female on Lisinopril for hypertension presented to emergency with rapidly progressing facial and tongue swelling. Airway management required.
Patient taking Metoprolol for atrial fibrillation developed symptomatic bradycardia with heart rate of 32 bpm requiring temporary pacing.
Female patient presented with jaundice and coagulopathy. History revealed intentional overdose of Paracetamol with total ingestion of 20g over 24 hours.
Elderly male on Digoxin for heart failure developed nausea, yellow-green visual disturbances and complete heart block. Serum digoxin level markedly elevated.
Young female on Isotretinoin for severe acne developed depressive symptoms with suicidal ideation within 6 weeks of treatment initiation.
Male patient developed TTP approximately two weeks after starting Clopidogrel post coronary stent placement. Plasmapheresis initiated.
Male patient developed widespread skin rash one day after starting Paracetamol for post-operative pain management.
Female patient developed agitation, hyperthermia, and clonus after Sertraline dose increase. Serotonin syndrome diagnosed and drug discontinued.
Hypertensive male on Furosemide presented with muscle weakness and paralysis. Potassium 2.1 mEq/L. Intravenous potassium replacement required.
Young female developed blistering mucocutaneous lesions covering 25% BSA after Penicillin course for streptococcal pharyngitis. ICU admission required.
Male patient on long-term Amiodarone for ventricular arrhythmia developed progressive dyspnoea and bilateral pulmonary infiltrates. Amiodarone-induced pulmonary toxicity confirmed.
Hyperthyroid female on Carbimazole presented with high fever and sore throat. WBC 0.8 x10^9/L. Agranulocytosis confirmed. Immediate drug withdrawal.
Male patient with mild dehydration developed oliguric acute kidney injury after high-dose Ibuprofen use for sports injury. Creatinine peaked at 5.2 mg/dL.
Elderly female on Methotrexate for rheumatoid arthritis developed elevated transaminases and biopsy confirmed methotrexate-induced hepatic fibrosis.

reaction: Drug Reaction with Eosinophilia and Systemic Symptoms
report_text: Male epileptic on Phenytoin developed fever, widespread rash, lymphadenopathy and eosinophilia meeting criteria for DRESS syndrome.
Female patient developed severe watery diarrhoea with abdominal cramps 5 days after completing Clindamycin course. C. difficile toxin positive.
Hypertensive male on Amlodipine developed bilateral pitting ankle oedema. Cardiac cause excluded. Drug-induced peripheral oedema confirmed.
Female patient on Venlafaxine ER developed severe headache with BP 210/120 mmHg. Hypertensive crisis attributed to norepinephrine reuptake inhibition.
Male presented with urticarial rash and pruritis starting 3 days after initiating Paracetamol 1g QDS for osteoarthritis flare.
Female on long-term Trimethoprim prophylaxis developed symptomatic anaemia. Blood film showed megaloblastic changes. Folate supplementation initiated.
Elderly male post-operative patient developed severe respiratory depression with RR 4/min after morphine patient-controlled analgesia. Naloxone administered.
Female epileptic on Carbamazepine presented with confusion and seizure recurrence. Sodium 118 mEq/L. SIADH secondary to Carbamazepine confirmed.
Male patient developed flushing, erythema and hypotension during Vancomycin infusion. Infusion rate slowed and antihistamine administered with resolution.
Female breast cancer patient on adjuvant Tamoxifen developed confirmed DVT in right femoral vein. Anticoagulation commenced.
Male gout patient developed severe mucocutaneous blistering reaction 3 weeks after starting Allopurinol. HLA-B*58:01 positive. Critical care admission.
Young female on combined oral contraceptive pill presented with pleuritic chest pain and dyspnoea. CTPA confirmed bilateral pulmonary emboli.
Male on Atorvastatin 80mg reported progressive proximal muscle weakness and myalgia. CK elevated 10x upper limit. Statin-induced myopathy confirmed.
Bipolar female on lithium carbonate developed coarse tremor, ataxia and confusion. Serum lithium 2.8 mmol/L. Haemodialysis performed.
Male heart failure patient on Spironolactone presented with weakness and ECG changes. Potassium 6.8 mEq/L. Emergency treatment with calcium gluconate and insulin.
Female patient reported maculopapular rash on trunk and upper limbs appearing 24 hours after starting Paracetamol for dental pain.
Male patient with depression developed manic episode with grandiosity and decreased sleep two weeks after Fluoxetine initiation.
Elderly female on long-term low-dose Aspirin presented with peritonitis. Emergency laparotomy confirmed perforated peptic ulcer.
TB patient on Rifampicin reported orange-red discolouration of urine, tears, and sweat. Patient counselled that this is an expected pharmacological effect.
Schizophrenic female on Clozapine surveillance detected absolute neutrophil count of 0.4 x10^9/L. Clozapine immediately withdrawn. Filgrastim initiated.
Male patient developed malaena and haematemesis after two weeks of Ibuprofen use. Endoscopy revealed bleeding duodenal ulcer requiring haemostasis.
Breast cancer patient developed symptomatic heart failure 6 months post-Doxorubicin chemotherapy. Echo confirmed dilated cardiomyopathy with EF 30%.
Type 2 diabetic male developed severe nausea, diarrhoea and abdominal cramps after initiating Metformin 500mg three times daily.
Female on Lamotrigine for bipolar disorder developed massive skin detachment affecting over 30% BSA. Burns unit admission. Mortality risk high.
Elderly male patient developed diffuse maculopapular rash over trunk and limbs 5 days into Amoxicillin course for community-acquired pneumonia.
Diabetic female on Rosiglitazone developed new-onset heart failure with bilateral peripheral oedema and pulmonary crackles. Drug discontinued.
Elderly male treated with Gentamicin for septicaemia developed bilateral sensorineural hearing loss. Audiogram confirmed permanent ototoxic damage.
Renal transplant female on Ciclosporin developed acute deterioration in graft function. Biopsy showed ciclosporin-induced nephrotoxicity.
Male patient on unfractionated Heparin for DVT developed paradoxical new thrombosis with platelet count falling to 42 x10^9/L. HIT antibodies positive.
Female patient developed itchy erythematous rash on arms and chest beginning 48 hours after Paracetamol use for headache.
Hypertensive male developed persistent non-productive dry cough 2 weeks after commencing Ramipril. Cough resolved completely upon switching to Losartan.
Asthmatic female inadvertently prescribed Bisoprolol developed acute bronchospasm requiring nebulised bronchodilator therapy in A&E.
Elderly male on Warfarin for AF developed retroperitoneal haematoma. INR supratherapeutic at 9.4. Vitamin K and PCC administered.
Female with inflammatory bowel disease developed drug-induced hepatitis within 6 weeks of Sulfasalazine initiation. LFTs normalised after withdrawal.
